required_artifacts.purpose: Wave-level stabilization inventory, protective cleanup inventory, and cleanup review ledger.
Repo-enforced semantic check that fails CI when required stabilization inventory or ledger fields drift.
Repo-level integration harness that locks main_loop_phases.cpp ordering and phase data-flow expectations.
Direct production-path touch gesture coverage for alert mute behavior and idle profile-cycle ordering.
Production-path characterization coverage for power module warning, shutdown, and auto power-off behavior.

remaining_gaps.description: Native suites now run one-at-a-time with isolated PlatformIO build roots instead of reusing a shared .pio/build/native artifact tree.

Legacy POST /api/settings has been removed. Device/AP/proxy/dev writes remain on POST /api/device/settings, the UI helper no longer defaults to /api/settings, and the route/doc contracts fail CI if the catch-all surface returns.
Risky seams previously covered only by wrappers or extracted harnesses now execute production code directly for power, touch, and main-loop phase ordering.
A repo-level integration harness now exercises main_loop_phases.cpp and fails on ordering or phase contract drift.
Tap gesture behavior is covered through the real TapGestureModule path, including alert mute and idle triple-tap profile cycling.

Wave 4 duplicate-owner consolidations are recorded in this manifest and enforced by CI; the targeted auto-push, alert-volume, Wi-Fi lifecycle, and speed-arbitration ownership cleanups are now landed and tracked here.

Loop phase context structs for connection-early, settings-prep, pre-ingest, ingest, display, post-display, runtime-snapshot, power-touch, and Wi-Fi runtime are now data-only; duplicate per-call callback override paths were removed in favor of begin()-configured provider ownership.

protective_cleanup_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Wi-Fi auto-start and lifecycle paths must keep attempted-versus-started semantics explicit and must not silently report success on degraded or blocked transitions.

protective_cleanup_inventory.current_owner: Runtime entrypoints and API services with shared-state dependencies
protective_cleanup_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Null or unavailable runtime dependencies continue to reject or short-circuit explicitly instead of dereferencing or silently mutating partial state.

protective_cleanup_inventory.current_owner: Mutation endpoints and maintenance services with active resource ownership
protective_cleanup_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Destructive or stateful operations continue to reject when logging, backup, restore, or storage ownership is active; cleanup may not weaken busy/in-progress semantics.

duplicate_owner_inventory.behavior: Auto-push execution across connect-time, profile-triggered, and explicit push-now requests.
duplicate_owner_inventory.intended_single_owner: AutoPushModule::process() owns all auto-push execution and retry state; every trigger must enter through AutoPushModule request/start APIs instead of duplicating execution logic elsewhere.
duplicate_owner_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Connect-time and manual push-now retries, perf counters, active profile selection, and BLE volume staging remain centralized; no trigger path may bypass the shared retry/error-reporting behavior or silently succeed.

duplicate_owner_inventory.behavior: Alert-time BLE volume transitions for alert fade/restore decisions.
current_owner_candidates: src/modules/volume_fade/volume_fade_module.cpp emits alert fade and restore actions
src/modules/display/display_orchestration_module.cpp executes BLE setVolume() for volume fade decisions
duplicate_owner_inventory.intended_single_owner: DisplayOrchestrationModule remains the sole BLE volume executor; VolumeFadeModule may only emit explicit decisions for it to arbitrate and execute.
duplicate_owner_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Alert fade/recovery semantics stay explicit, BLE pacing failures remain visible, and the loop must not issue competing or duplicate setVolume() commands in the same alert path.

duplicate_owner_inventory.behavior: Wi-Fi AP/STA/AP+STA start, re-enable, and reconnect lifecycle transitions.
current_owner_candidates: src/main.cpp touch/manual Wi-Fi start callback -> WiFiManager::startSetupMode(false)
src/wifi_client.cpp WiFiManager::processWifiClientConnectPhase() STA reconnect and AP+STA transition path
duplicate_owner_inventory.intended_single_owner: WiFiManager lifecycle state owns AP/STA/AP+STA transitions; WifiAutoStartModule and touch/manual requesters call WiFiManager::startSetupMode(...), while WifiOrchestratorModule is limited to callback binding and must not implement parallel lifecycle mutations.
duplicate_owner_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Attempted-versus-started reporting, blocked transition visibility, AP client grace behavior, and fail-open safety remain explicit; callback binding still occurs even when Wi-Fi was already started elsewhere, and cleanup does not silently collapse blocked or degraded Wi-Fi transitions into success.

duplicate_owner_inventory.behavior: Selection of the committed current-loop speed source (OBD only) and status consumed by display and Wi-Fi surfaces.
current_owner_candidates: src/modules/obd/obd_api_service.cpp and src/modules/wifi/backup_api_service.cpp sync selector enable inputs only via speedSourceSelector.syncEnabledInputs(obdEnabled)
Downstream consumers such as display and Wi-Fi modules may only read selectedSpeed()/snapshot() and must not reinterpret raw OBD runtime state as a fallback selector
duplicate_owner_inventory.intended_single_owner: speedSourceSelector.update(now) remains the sole mutating speed-selection owner; config and restore writes may only sync selector enable inputs through speedSourceSelector.syncEnabledInputs(obdEnabled), and downstream modules consume selectedSpeed()/snapshot() or pure snapshotAt(nowMs) reads without reinterpreting source state.
duplicate_owner_inventory.preserved_negative_semantics: Current-loop selection still happens before every consumer read, unavailable speed stays explicit instead of falling back to stale raw runtime data, and config/restore writes update the selector input without re-arbitrating committed selection in-place.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Production main_loop_phases always drove early-loop execution through begin()-configured providers, so the per-call callback fields in LoopConnectionEarlyContext were duplicate behavior channels with no production owner.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Missing providers still leave the phase in a safe zeroed state, and request-show scanning semantics remain explicit even when no show handler is configured.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Tap gesture execution and settings snapshot reads are now owned solely by the module's configured providers; main_loop_phases never set per-call overrides in LoopSettingsPrepContext.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: An absent tap or settings provider remains safe: tap work is skipped and the settings snapshot falls back to conservative defaults.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Boot-ready gate opening, Wi-Fi priority application, and debug API ticks are now dispatched only through begin()-configured providers; the per-call override fields in LoopPreIngestContext were a duplicate path not used by production.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Replay mode still suppresses boot-open and Wi-Fi progression while preserving debug processing, and empty handlers still short-circuit safely.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: LoopRuntimeSnapshotContext is now a pure phase marker while BLE/DMA/preview reads come only from the configured providers, matching the production main_loop_phases contract.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Missing snapshot providers still yield explicit false/default values instead of re-reading or mutating hidden fallback state.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Power processing and touch UI evaluation are now owned only by begin()-configured providers; the per-call callback fields in LoopPowerTouchContext duplicated production behavior without adding a distinct owner.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Settings-mode early return, wrap-safe timing, DMA refresh, and heap-stat capture remain explicit and test-covered, while missing providers still no-op safely.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: BLE process and queue drain execution are now provider-owned through begin()-configured hooks; the per-call runBleProcess and runBleDrain fields in LoopIngestContext duplicated production execution without a separate owner.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Disabling BLE process for a loop still skips only the process step while drain and backpressure merge behavior remain explicit and test-covered.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Display pipeline dispatch is owned solely by begin()-configured providers, and the former LoopDisplayResult.signalPriorityActive output had no production consumer after processLoopDisplayPreWifiPhase stopped exporting mutable phase state. The module now exposes only its owned side effects.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Parsed-frame gating still controls whether the pipeline runs, lightweight refresh still receives the pipeline-ran signal, and missing providers still no-op safely without exporting a test-only result channel.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Auto-push execution and connection-state dispatch are now owned only by the module's configured providers; the per-call callback fields in LoopPostDisplayContext were dead duplicate behavior channels in production.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Context fallback data for dispatch timing and BLE connection state remains intact when read providers are absent, and enableAutoPush/runSpeedAndDispatch still gate the side effects explicitly.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Wi-Fi manager processing is now owned only by begin()-configured providers; the per-call runWifiManagerProcess field in WifiRuntimeContext duplicated the production Wi-Fi progression path without a distinct owner.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Skip-late-non-core, policy gating, cadence throttling, and visual sync remain explicit and test-covered, while missing providers still short-circuit safely without silently reporting Wi-Fi processing success.

cleanup_review_ledger.current_owner: AutoPushModule queueSlotPush()/queuePushNow() request path plus AutoPushModule::process() executor
cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Connect-time selection, tap-triggered slot cycling, manual profile pushes, and explicit push-now requests now all queue through AutoPushModule request APIs before the shared AutoPushModule::process() executor runs. The injected profilePushFn/manual start path was removed, and start() no longer arms state independently of the shared queue rules.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Connect-time default-slot pushes, tap-triggered slot changes, display progress indicators, explicit push-now retry metrics, and active in-progress rejection remain explicit; new requests can no longer clobber an in-flight push.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Alert volume-fade decisions flow into DisplayOrchestrationModule for BLE volume execution. DisplayOrchestrationModule remains the sole BLE volume executor.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Fade/restore logic remains explicit and independent, and the alert path can no longer issue duplicate BLE volume writes in the same parsed-frame pass.

cleanup_review_ledger.current_owner: WiFiManager lifecycle state with boot-time callback binding via WifiOrchestrator::ensureCallbacksConfigured()
cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: WifiOrchestrator::startWifi() was removed. Boot auto-start and touch/manual entrypoints now request WiFiManager::startSetupMode(...) directly, while WifiOrchestrator only binds Wi-Fi callbacks exactly once through ensureCallbacksConfigured(). This deletes the duplicate lifecycle mutation path without replacing the manager state machine.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Auto-start still distinguishes attempted from started-successfully, manual/touch re-enable still goes through the real WiFiManager admission checks, and callback binding remains explicit and idempotent even if Wi-Fi was already started elsewhere before the orchestrator runs.

cleanup_review_ledger.current_owner: SpeedSourceSelector enabled-input sync via syncEnabledInputs() plus SpeedSourceSelector::update() committed selection
cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: Production write paths now only sync OBD selector input. OBD config and backup restore now sync the selector input through speedSourceSelector.syncEnabledInputs(obdEnabled), while speedSourceSelector.update(now) remains the only committed arbitration step.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: Config and restore writes still update enable inputs immediately for pure snapshotAt/status reads, but they do not re-arbitrate the committed source in-place; consumers still see committed selection only after the loop-owned update() pass and may not fall back to raw OBD runtime data.

cleanup_review_ledger.replacement_or_deadness_proof: The legacy POST /api/settings catch-all route and WifiSettingsApiService::handleApiSettingsSave() have been removed. Existing Svelte clients already saved device settings through POST /api/device/settings. The shared UI helper no longer defaults to /api/settings, and the WiFi route contracts/docs are updated to reject drift back to the catch-all surface.
cleanup_review_ledger.preserved_negative_semantics: AP credential validation and device-setting persistence remain under WifiSettingsApiService::handleApiDeviceSettingsSave(); cleanup does not silently drop writes or weaken protected rejection behavior.
